Title: Gymnosperm-specific CYP90Js enable biflavonoid biosynthesis and microbial production of amentoflavone

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ginkgo biloba; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes intermolecular oxidative C3'-C8'' coupling of two apigenin molecules to form amentoflavone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for biflavonoid scaffold formation; in the QM/MM mechanism the second HAT step is rate-limiting within the catalytic cycle
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in leaves of Ginkgo biloba
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with apigenin feeding; heterologous E. coli expression with substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: tissue metabolite profiling; RNA-seq co-occurrence with leaf accumulation; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; heterologous expression in E. coli; LC-HRMS/MS product identification with authentic standard; kinetic analysis; mutagenesis; MD and QM/MM calculations
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbCYP90J6 was identified from leaf-enriched candidates and uniquely converted apigenin to amentoflavone. 2. It catalyzes regioselective intermolecular C3'-C8'' flavone dimerization and prefers non-methylated apigenin. 3. It is the core enzyme initiating ginkgo amentoflavone-type biflavonoid biosynthesis and enabled microbial amentoflavone production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of amentoflavone from apigenin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neuroprotective and therapeutic biflavonoid scaffold with anti-amyloid and multi-target activities
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector in tobacco; pFe00/pBIF21 heterologous expression in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, CRA019958; metabolomics/LC-MS; Figshare mass spectrometry dataset 10.6084/m9.figshare.29552828.v1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for metabolic engineering of amentoflavone-type biflavonoids and microbial biomanufacturing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gymnosperm-specific CYP90J orthologs correlate with biflavonoid-producing species; does not accept methylated apigenins 6 or 7; also weakly dimerizes naringenin and trace tricetin

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers methyl group to phenolic hydroxyls of amentoflavone derivatives, preferring 4'-OH and weakly 7''-OH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct methylated derivatives of the biflavonoid scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro crude enzyme assay with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli lysate; LC-MS/MS; product comparison with standards and NMR-based site assignment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbOMT1 methylates amentoflavone to bilobetin. 2. It shows regioselective preference for 4'-OH and limited activity at 7''-OH. 3. It can further convert bilobetin to a low-yield tentative dimethylated product 10.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of methylated amentoflavone derivatives, especially bilobetin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive biflavonoid derivatives in Ginkgo extract
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tailoring methylation patterns of biflavonoids in synthetic biology

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Selectively methylates the 4'-OH position of amentoflavone to produce bilobetin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms methylated derivative of target scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro crude enzyme assay with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; recombinant enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbOMT2 converts amentoflavone to bilobetin. 2. It is regioselective for the 4'-OH position. 3. It acts in the post-dimerization methylation branch of ginkgo biflavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive biflavonoid derivative in Ginkgo extract
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enables targeted production of bilobetin in engineered systems

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Selectively methylates the 7-OH position, converting bilobetin to ginkgetin and amentoflavone to sequoiaflavone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms methylated derivatives of the biflavonoid scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro crude enzyme assay with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; recombinant enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; NOESY/standard comparison for product assignment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbOMT3 converts bilobetin to ginkgetin. 2. It also methylates amentoflavone to sequoiaflavone. 3. Its regioselectivity is mainly toward the 7-OH position.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive methylated biflavonoid derivatives in Ginkgo extract
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for producing specific downstream methylated biflavonoids

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Selectively methylates the 4'''-OH position, converting bilobetin to isoginkgetin and amentoflavone to podocarpusflavone A
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms methylated derivatives of the biflavonoid scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro crude enzyme assay with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; recombinant enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; NOESY/standard comparison for product assignment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbOMT4 converts bilobetin to isoginkgetin. 2. It also methylates amentoflavone to podocarpusflavone A. 3. It is selective for the 4'''-OH position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoginkgetin and podocarpusflavone A formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive methylated biflavonoid derivatives in Ginkgo extract
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway branching toward isoginkgetin-type products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with GbOMT5 for some products

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Selectively methylates the 4'''-OH position, converting bilobetin to isoginkgetin and amentoflavone to podocarpusflavone A
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms methylated derivatives of the biflavonoid scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro crude enzyme assay with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-expression analysis; recombinant enzyme assay; LC-MS/MS; NOESY/standard comparison for product assignment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GbOMT5 converts bilobetin to isoginkgetin. 2. It also methylates amentoflavone to podocarpusflavone A. 3. It is selective for the 4'''-OH position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoginkgetin and podocarpusflavone A formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive methylated biflavonoid derivatives in Ginkgo extract
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway branching toward isoginkgetin-type products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with GbOMT4 for some products

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes intermolecular oxidative dimerization of apigenin to amentoflavone
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterologous E. coli expression with substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in E. coli; LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChCYP90J7 is a functional gymnosperm CYP90J that dimerizes apigenin to amentoflavone. 2. It prefers apigenin over naringenin. 3. It supports functional conservation of biflavonoid synthase activity in some gymnosperm CYP90Js.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pFe00 heterologous expression in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative enzyme source for synthetic biology of biflavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Higher catalytic efficiency toward apigenin than naringenin

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative dimerization of naringenin but not apigenin under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms dimeric flavonoid products related to biflavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterologous E. coli expression with substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in E. coli; LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MgCYP90J1 did not dimerize apigenin under tested conditions. 2. It catalyzed only naringenin dimerization. 3. This indicates substrate diversification within gymnosperm CYP90Js.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pFe00 heterologous expression in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential biocatalyst for alternative flavonoid dimers
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural end products not structurally resolved due to low conversion

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Weakly catalyzes oxidative dimerization of apigenin and naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterologous E. coli expression with substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in E. coli; LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmCYP90E3v1 showed weak activity toward apigenin and naringenin dimerization. 2. It provides functional support for the proposed evolutionary origin of gymnosperm CYP90Js from lycophyte CYP90Es. 3. Activity was much weaker than GbCYP90J6.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces trace amentoflavone and trace naringenin dimer
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pFe00 heterologous expression in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Evolutionary enzyme template for engineering improved dimerases
